native_name: กะเหรี่ยง (กะยา)
textile_history: Karen women are renowned for their backstrap loom weaving, producing distinctive striped textiles. The famous 'Karen skirt' (sinh) features bold horizontal bands. Traditional weaving is passed from mother to daughter.
cultural_significance: Textiles serve as ethnic identity markers. Specific stripe patterns indicate clan affiliation, marital status, and social rank. The iconic red-black-white color scheme is deeply symbolic.
